slug: 黑客到底在做什么_不是电影里那样的
title: 黑客到底在做什么——不是电影里那样的

outline.concept: Real hacking is mostly running pre-built tools (Metasploit, Nmap scripts, exploit kits) against known vulnerabilities — more like picking locks with a master key set than inventing a new way through walls. Most attackers never write original exploits.
outline.query: 用现成工具 模板 不自己写 复用 套框架

outline.concept: The majority of real-world breaches exploit CVEs that already have patches available. Hackers bet on organizations not updating — they're mining update laziness, not discovering zero-days. Unpatched systems are the low-hanging fruit that actually gets picked.
outline.query: 没更新 懒得升级 补丁 旧版本 延迟 系统

outline.concept: Social engineering often bypasses all technical security entirely — phishing emails, impersonation calls, tailgating through doors. The most effective 'hack' is convincing a human to hand over access voluntarily. No firewall stops someone who already has the password.
outline.query: 被骗 伪装 钓鱼 冒充 点链接 交出密码 信任

outline.concept: Most actual hacking time is reconnaissance and waiting — scanning networks, gathering public info (OSINT), running automated scripts that ping thousands of targets. It's patient, boring, repetitive work punctuated by brief moments of access, not a dramatic real-time code battle.
outline.query: 等待 观察 收集信息 耐心 扫描 重复 无聊
